LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER EUGENE YOSHI MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: APPLICATION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE DENIED. PETITIONER HAS NOT YET EXHAUSTED ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. RESPONDENT HAS NOT ISSUED A FINAL DECISION AND RETAINS JURISDICTION OVER THIS MATTER. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 1936.5 DOES NOT APPLY TO PETITIONER WHO IS A CITY EMPLOYEE. NO DUE PROCESS VIOLATION BECAUSE PETITIONER FAILS TO SHOW PREJUDICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Memorandum Ofp/A In Support Thereof;
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER EUGENE YOSHI Motion for Writ of Mandate. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Application for Writ of Mandate denied. Petitioner has not yet exhausted administrative remedies. Respondent has not issued a final decision and retains jurisdiction over this matter. Government Code section 1936.5 does not apply to Petitioner who is a City employee. No due process violation because petitioner fails to show prejudice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
